title: Patterns of medication use across society from national primary care dispensing data
data_sources: NHSBSA primary care dispensing data (NHS England SDE)
GDPPR (General Practice Extraction Service Data for Pandemic Planning and Research)
NHS SDE curated demographics asset (BHF Data Science Centre)

data_scope.polypharmacy_threshold: 5+ distinct chemical substances in 84 consecutive days
data_scope.data_access: restricted to accredited researchers via NHS DARS

core_conclusion: Analysing 5.8 billion primary care medication dispensings for 52.6 million individuals in England (November 2019 – December 2024), this study shows the COVID-19 pandemic disrupted medication initiation differently across drug classes: gastrointestinal and mental health medications remained persistently below pre-pandemic levels, while cardiovascular and diabetes medications recovered and exceeded them. Stark inequities are revealed: women receive up to twice the antidepressant volume of men in young adulthood; Bangladeshi and Pakistani groups have dispensing rate ratios up to 2.5-fold higher by age 60; the most deprived quintile has rates twice those of the least deprived from age 40. By age 70, 42% of the population are on five or more concurrent medications.
why_it_matters: Unprecedented population-scale evidence covering 52.6 million individuals and 5.8 billion dispensings; quantifies which pandemic-disrupted clinical pathways have and have not recovered; reveals intersecting health inequalities across sex, ethnicity, and deprivation simultaneously; frames polypharmacy as a systemic NHS challenge; delivers a living dashboard for near-real-time monitoring of prescribing trends.
why_nature: Whole-population linked data at a scale rarely achieved in medicines research; directly policy-translatable to NHS commissioning and NICE priorities; explicit quantification of ethnic and socioeconomic disparities fits Nature Health's health equity mandate; CVD-COVID-UK framework presented as a replicable model for other countries; open-source code and public dashboard constitute reproducible, updatable research infrastructure.
added_value_to_my_research: Spatialize LSOA-level dispensing surfaces as outcome for GWR/MGWR models linking GP accessibility (R2SFCA) to deprivation-dispensing gradients
Join ethnic dispensing RRs to R2SFCA primary care accessibility scores to test whether access barriers explain ethnic medication disparities
Incorporate mobile phone mobility covariates into Poisson dispensing models to disentangle pandemic access reduction from genuine disease incidence decline
Apply SHAP-based explainable ML to BNF longitudinal trajectories to predict and map polypharmacy escalation risk at neighbourhood level
Design BNF-to-ATC federated harmonization layer for cross-country comparison of deprivation-dispensing inequality using FAIR data principles

related.title: Developing a novel index for neighborhood social determinants of cardiovascular diseases in the CARDIA study
related.relation: neighbourhood-level social determinant indexing for cardiovascular disease — extends the paper's deprivation-dispensing gradient to spatial mapping

related.title: Unequal spatial patterns and differentiated city roles in the intercity telemedicine system
related.relation: spatial inequity in healthcare access — directly relevant to whether ethnic dispensing disparities are access-mediated

related.title: Mobility network models of COVID-19 explain inequities and inform reopening
related.relation: mobility-based COVID inequity modelling — provides methodological basis for incorporating mobility into the paper's Poisson dispensing models

related.title: Access to care affects electronic health record reliability and AI-driven disease prediction
related.relation: healthcare access disparities in EHR data quality — directly relevant to interpreting the paper's ethnicity and deprivation dispensing signals

related.title: Decomposition of cross-country socioeconomic inequality in mortality by 288 causes of death and 84 risk factors from 1990 to 2021
related.relation: cross-country socioeconomic inequality in health outcomes — provides the comparative international framework for the paper's proposed federated medicines intelligence extension

figures.caption: Incident rates of medications dispensed per 1,000 person-months by BNF chapter (1–15) and sex, November 2019 – December 2024 — pandemic disruption and recovery trajectories across drug classes
Medication dispensing in England in 2024 by BNF chapter and sex — count and rate by age showing the sharp polypharmacy rise from the 40s onward
Top 10 most frequently dispensed chemical substances in England by age band and sex in 2024 — sertraline dominates young adults with 2x volume in women; CVD drugs dominate male middle age
Log rate ratio heatmaps of medication dispensing by ethnic group vs. white British baseline — Bangladeshi and Pakistani groups show RRs up to 2.5 by age 60
Log rate ratio heatmaps of medication dispensing by IMD deprivation quintile vs. least deprived — most deprived quintile shows 2-fold higher rates from age 40
Polypharmacy trends by age (2024 cross-section) and over time (November 2019 – December 2024) — 15% at age 50, 42% at age 70
